codeReview.summary: Implementation matches the feature contract: ThemeContext now supports papierklammer/violet-indigo/earth with localStorage persistence, sidebar and Instance Settings selectors are present, and theme application is global via html[data-theme]. No blocking correctness issues found in the reviewed diff.

issues.description: The added tests are mostly source-string checks (fs.readFileSync + expect(source).toContain(...)) rather than runtime behavior tests. They do not assert that ThemeProvider actually applies data-theme or persists/rehydrates selected theme at runtime, so regression detection for key behavior is weak.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The gui-worker procedure and actual worker behavior diverged on validation scope/TDD ordering: skill requires tests-first and includes running TUI tests, but transcript shows implementation edits before test creation and only UI tests + typecheck were run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/gui-worker/SKILL.md step 3 ('Write tests FIRST') and step 6 (includes `pnpm exec vitest run packages/orchestrator-tui/ --max-workers=3`) vs handoff verification commands listing only `pnpm exec vitest run ui/src/ --max-workers=3` and `pnpm -r typecheck`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Agent-browser CLI usage guidance appears incomplete in shared state; worker spent multiple attempts discovering valid command syntax (`navigate --url` failed, then `execute`/`js` failed, then `eval` succeeded).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session d88bf235-ce1c-478c-8688-066511b8f17b shows failed agent-browser invocations (`navigate --url`, `execute`, `js`) followed by `agent-browser --help` and eventual `eval` usage.

summary: Pass. The feature behavior is implemented end-to-end and aligns with expected assertions; only a non-blocking automated-test quality gap and shared-state/skill documentation gaps were identified.
